title: Add podman run --gpus flag for compatibility
body:
People who use docker scripts with Podman see failures
if they use `podman run --gpus` flag. This is a noop flag.

Resolves https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/10420

comments.body:
Looks like I need to either not have docs (man pages) or make the flag visible to `--help` (not hidden).
Which is the preferred outcome?

Also, do we want a test for this flag given it's a noop?

Thanks for the review @TomSweeneyRedHat, it seems the problem I'm running into now is that the CI does not allow me to mark the flags hidden while also adding documentation to the man files. However, I do think that the documentation should be added in some place.
AFAICT without the OCI hooks installed the GPU support will not work, so users adding this flag may be curious why it doesn't work for them.

@sjug Don't document them.  We don't want users to know about them.  The only goal is if they have a script built with docker in mind, or with podman-docker installed, it completes successfully, but we don't want to have users thinking the option is useful with podman.

@sjug: Cannot trigger testing until a trusted user reviews the PR and leaves an `/ok-to-test` message.

<details>

In response to [this](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/10430#issuecomment-848336239):

>/retest


Instructions for interacting with me using PR comments are available [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md).  If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes/test-infra](https://github.com/kubernetes/test-infra/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.
</details>

@sjug one test failed with absolutely no bread crumbs, just restarted.
Otherwise LGTM
